serviceNotes.engine oil: PHEV models still require petrol engine maintenance on Mercedes EU schedules (typically 15,000 km or 12 months for oil when the combustion engine runs enough to reach distance limits—ASSYST adjusts for hybrid use).
serviceNotes.dust filter: Cabin dust filter per Mercedes maintenance booklet (same as other W213 variants).
serviceNotes.engine air filter: Petrol engine air filter at standard ASSYST inspection intervals.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: 24-month brake fluid renewal per Mercedes-Benz European requirements for W213.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: Hybrid regenerative braking extends pad life but inspections remain mandatory at each service.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: Petrol engine spark plugs on M274-based PHEV powertrains follow standard extended intervals (60,000 km / 48 months EU orientation—verify booklet).
serviceNotes.coolant: Engine cooling circuit coolant per Mercedes long-life interval (near 150,000 km).
serviceNotes.high-voltage battery coolant: Mercedes specifies separate HV battery/e-drive coolant circuit maintenance on plug-in hybrids; 120,000 km is a commonly documented major service point—always use a qualified EV workshop and confirm in the high-voltage supplement for your model year.
serviceNotes.automatic transmission fluid: 9G-TRONIC integrated with hybrid motor: preventive ATF service near 60,000 km is a common specialist recommendation.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: Supplementary engine coolant exchange at 120,000 km if not yet due for OEM long interval.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Direct-injection petrol PHEV engines can still accumulate intake deposits; address around 120,000 km if symptoms appear.
